title: SERVICES AGAINST EXPLOITATION - 211info
text: IRCO PACIFIC ISLANDER AND ASIAN FAMILY CENTER SERVICES AGAINST EXPLOITATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services Against Exploitation (SAE) serves victims and survivors of all forms of human trafficking with an emphasis on foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee victims and survivors. A team of trauma-informed advocates and staff provides culturally and linguistically responsive case management, immigration legal assistance, and community referrals. They meet survivors where they are and offer survivor-centered, wrap-around services throughout each survivor’s journey toward self-empowerment and liberation. 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Survivors and victims of all forms of human trafficking. Culturally and linguistically specific services available for foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee survivors. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5:30pm Email (971) 312-9602 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Program Phone (971) 312-9602 Main (503) 234-1541 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, Russian, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus lines 12 and 72 Services: Case/Care Management * Human Trafficking Issues Immigration/Naturalization Legal Services * Human Trafficking Issues Specialized Information and Referral * Human Trafficking Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AND FINANCE - ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH ACADEMIC SUPPORT AND PARENT ENGAGEMENT HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - HEALTH NAVIGATION HOUSING/SHELTER - EMERGENCY RENTAL ASSISTANCE HOUSING/SHELTER - RENT ASSISTANCE FOR FAMILIES WITH CHILDREN INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - ASIAN AND PACIFIC ISLANDER DEVELOPMENT INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILDREN'S PROGRAMS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - YOUTH MENTORING LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - GANG PREVENTION AND INTERVENTION MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - FAMILY PROGRAMS UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
